title: Marketplace Workspace-Template Contract
description: SSOT JSON-Schema (draft 2020-12) for the Molecule marketplace workspace-template artifact — the publishable shape of a workspace-template `config.yaml` (molecule-ai-workspace-template-* repos). DERIVED FROM THE REAL ARTIFACTS + their CI validator: molecule-ai-workspace-template-claude-code/config.yaml, google-adk, seo-agent, platform-agent, validated by validate-workspace-template.py (required: name, runtime, template_schema_version; runtime warns if outside the known set {langgraph, claude-code, crewai, autogen, deepagents, hermes, gemini-cli, openclaw}). DELIBERATELY TOLERANT (additionalProperties:true at the top and on nested objects): the real configs are SHAPE-INCONSISTENT about where the model lives — google-adk has top-level `models[]` + `runtime_config.model`; claude-code/seo-agent have `runtime_config.models[]` + top-level `model`; platform-agent has top-level `provider`; some have none. This schema models every observed location WITHOUT forcing one, so a real config in any of those styles validates. The two `providers` shapes are also inconsistent and are typed separately by path: top-level `providers[]` is a list of provider OBJECTS (name/auth_mode/model_prefixes/...); `runtime_config.providers[]` is a list of provider-name STRINGS. `runtime` uses the CANONICAL hyphen form (see plugin-manifest.schema.json for the runtimes SSOT reconciliation).

name.description: Template display name (required by validate-workspace-template.py). E.g. `Claude Code Agent`, `SEO Agent`.

runtime.description: Runtime this template targets (required). Canonical hyphen form; the validator warns (does not reject) on values outside its known set.

template_schema_version.description: Template schema version (required; validate-workspace-template.py errors if missing — `template_schema_version: 1`).

model.description: Optional default model id (top-level form, e.g. `moonshot/kimi-k2.6`). One of several model locations — see schema description.

provider.description: Optional default provider name (top-level form, e.g. `platform` — platform-agent).

providers.description: Top-level provider registry: a list of provider OBJECTS (claude-code adapter provider contract). NOTE: distinct from `runtime_config.providers`, which is a list of provider-name strings.

models.description: Top-level `models[]` — the authoritative servability list for langgraph/google-adk-style runtimes (google-adk). The registry projection is a subset of this.

prompt_files.description: Prompt files delivered to the workspace (e.g. `system-prompt.md`, `prompts/seo-agent.md`).

items.description: A prompt file path within the template repo.

plugins.description: Declared plugins as source-contract strings the workspace-server gitea resolver understands: `gitea://<owner>/<repo>[/<subpath>]#<ref>`. Installed dynamically post-boot by the reconcile (RFC#2843 #32), NOT via the provisioning channel.

skills.description: Bare skill names enabled for the template (e.g. `seo`, `research`).

tools.description: Bare tool names enabled for the template (optional).

schedules.description: Default schedule grid seeded into workspace_schedules with source='template' at provision time (mirrors org_import.go). Runtime-added schedules are preserved across re-provisions; only source='template' rows are refreshed.

env.description: Required/optional environment variable declarations for the workspace.

memory.description: Optional memory configuration. Shape varies by runtime; modelled permissively (open object).

delegation.description: Optional delegation configuration. Modelled permissively (open object).

sandbox.description: Optional sandbox configuration. Modelled permissively (open object).

sub_workspaces.description: Optional sub-workspace declarations. Modelled permissively (items are open objects).

runtimeId.description: Canonical runtime identifier (hyphen form). Same SSOT enum as plugin-manifest.schema.json; underscore aliases accepted.

provider.description: A provider entry in the top-level `providers[]` registry (claude-code adapter provider contract).

model_prefixes.description: Model id prefixes routed to this provider (e.g. `kimi-`, `claude-`).

model_aliases.description: Model aliases routed to this provider (e.g. `sonnet`, `opus`, `haiku`).

base_url.description: Provider base URL. Loosely typed: a string OR null (seo-agent uses `base_url: null` for key-only providers).

auth_token_env.description: The single env var holding the bearer token for this provider.

model.description: A servable model entry (used in both top-level `models[]` and `runtime_config.models[]`).

name.description: Human-readable display name shown in the model dropdown.

required_env.description: Env vars the user must supply for this model (empty for platform-managed/keyless).

cron_expr.description: Cron expression (5-field), in tenant-local clock time.

timezone.description: IANA timezone string (literal; the seeder does not expand env vars here).

prompt.description: Inline prompt run on the cron tick.

prompt_file.description: Path to a prompt file run on the cron tick (alternative to inline `prompt`).

required.description: Env vars required at provision time.

runtimeConfig.description: Runtime-specific configuration. Tolerant (open) because the model/provider surface varies across runtimes.

model.description: Default model id for the runtime.

provider.description: Default provider name for the runtime.

providers.description: Provider NAMES enabled for the runtime (list of STRINGS — distinct from the top-level `providers[]` object list).

models.description: Servable models for the runtime (same model entry shape as top-level `models[]`).

required_env.description: Env vars required by the runtime.

recommended_env.description: Env vars recommended (not required) by the runtime.
